predictions.prediction_text: Donald Trump becomes President of the United States, leaving a massive budget deficit for his successor
predictions.actual_event: Donald Trump elected 45th President in 2016, re-elected in 2024
predictions.notes: Written as satire of Trump's 2000 Reform Party exploratory run. Writer Dan Greaney confirmed intent was satirical, not prophetic.

predictions.episode_title: When You Dish Upon a Star
predictions.prediction_text: Disney acquires 20th Century Fox
predictions.actual_event: Disney completed $71B acquisition of 21st Century Fox assets in 2019
predictions.notes: A sign reading '20th Century Fox, a Division of Walt Disney Co.' appears as a throwaway gag. Eerily precise.

predictions.prediction_text: Siegfried and Roy are attacked by their white tiger during a live performance
predictions.actual_event: Roy Horn mauled by white Bengal tiger on stage in October 2003
predictions.notes: The show's parody duo Gunter and Ernst are attacked during their act — mirrors the real 2003 incident almost exactly.

predictions.prediction_text: Wrist-worn communication devices with screens become mainstream
predictions.actual_event: Apple Watch launched 2014; Samsung Gear in 2013
predictions.notes: Show predicted 2010; actual smartwatches arrived 2013-2014. Close but not exact on timeline.

predictions.prediction_text: Video calling becomes a standard form of communication
predictions.actual_event: Apple FaceTime launched 2010, exactly as predicted
predictions.notes: The 2010 timeframe was actually correct for FaceTime.

predictions.prediction_text: Washington Redskins win Super Bowl XXVI
predictions.actual_event: Washington Redskins defeated Buffalo Bills in Super Bowl XXVI, January 1992
predictions.notes: Episode aired days before the game. Subsequent reruns were re-dubbed with the correct teams each year — Dallas Cowboys in 1993, also correct.

predictions.episode_title: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
predictions.prediction_text: FIFA officials arrested on corruption and bribery charges
predictions.actual_event: 14 FIFA officials indicted by U.S. Department of Justice in May 2015
predictions.notes: Episode also correctly predicted Germany defeating Brazil in the 2014 World Cup.

predictions.episode_title: You Don't Have to Live Like a Referee
predictions.prediction_text: Germany defeats Brazil in the World Cup
predictions.actual_event: Germany defeated Brazil 7-1 in 2014 World Cup semi-final
predictions.notes: Same episode that predicted the FIFA scandal.

predictions.prediction_text: Bengt Holmström wins the Nobel Prize in Economics
predictions.actual_event: Bengt Holmström won Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences in 2016
predictions.notes: Named by name on a betting card. Holmström was a respected MIT economist but the specific Nobel prediction is remarkable.

predictions.prediction_text: U.S. Olympic curling team beats Sweden to win gold
predictions.actual_event: U.S. men's curling team defeated Sweden 10-7 at 2018 Winter Olympics in Pyeongchang
predictions.notes: Got the exact opponent AND the upset result correct. One of the highest-specificity hits in the dataset.

predictions.prediction_text: A viral illness originating in east Asia spreads globally, causing mass panic and an epidemic in the United States
predictions.actual_event: COVID-19 pandemic originated in Wuhan, China in 2019-2020
predictions.notes: The 'Osaka Flu' episode. Writer Bill Oakley has said the comparison is coincidental — flu pandemics from Asia are a recurring historical pattern, not a prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: Homer's blackboard equation approximates the mass of the Higgs boson particle
predictions.actual_event: Higgs boson confirmed by CERN in 2012
predictions.notes: Dr. Simon Singh verified the equation. Writer David X. Cohen (later of Futurama) had a physics background. Educated extrapolation, not magic.

predictions.prediction_text: Cypress Hill performs with the London Symphony Orchestra
predictions.actual_event: Cypress Hill performed with the LSO at Royal Albert Hall on July 10, 2024
predictions.notes: The joke in the episode was that someone ordered the LSO 'possibly while high.' The real performance was also at the LSO's initiative.

predictions.episode_title: The Ziff Who Came to Dinner
predictions.prediction_text: A fourth Matrix film is released with a Christmas/holiday premiere
predictions.actual_event: The Matrix Resurrections released December 22, 2021
predictions.notes: Poster for 'Matrix 4' shown in background. Released Christmas week exactly as the background gag implied.

predictions.prediction_text: NSA conducts mass domestic surveillance on American citizens
predictions.actual_event: Edward Snowden revealed NSA PRISM surveillance program in 2013
predictions.notes: Also shown in The Simpsons Movie (2007). Government surveillance was an existing concern; this is more satire-meets-reality than prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: Robotic/automated systems malfunction and turn against humans
predictions.notes: Very vague — animatronic robots at a theme park go haywire. Fans connect this to AI concerns in 2025. Speculative stretch.

predictions.prediction_text: Autocorrect on smartphones misinterprets typed messages in absurd ways
predictions.actual_event: iPhone autocorrect errors became a cultural phenomenon after 2007
predictions.notes: The Apple Newton autocorrect joke dates to S04E14 (1992). Snopes notes the Newton was a real 1993 device notorious for bad handwriting recognition — not a prediction of iPhone.

predictions.prediction_text: A real minor league baseball team called the Albuquerque Isotopes is established
predictions.actual_event: The Albuquerque Isotopes officially adopted the name in 2003, after fan demand inspired by the episode
predictions.notes: Self-fulfilling prophecy — the team actually named themselves after the Simpsons episode.

predictions.prediction_text: The Shard skyscraper is built near London's Big Ben
predictions.actual_event: The Shard completed in 2012, visible from near Big Ben, with a similar shape and position as shown
predictions.notes: A pointed spire visible on the London skyline in a background shot. The Shard's position and shape match closely.

predictions.prediction_text: Lady Gaga performs at the Super Bowl halftime show suspended from cables
predictions.actual_event: Lady Gaga performed Super Bowl LI halftime show in February 2017, descending from the stadium roof
predictions.notes: Snopes rated this mixed — the show depicted Gaga performing suspended, but it was not at the Super Bowl in the episode. Some images circulated were also doctored.

predictions.prediction_text: Bender from Futurama predicts the 2014 midterm elections
predictions.actual_event: No verified connection
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — This is a fan fabrication with no basis in the actual crossover episode.

predictions.prediction_text: U.S. government officially acknowledges the existence of extraterrestrial life
predictions.notes: Fans in early 2026 began speculating this could happen as UAP congressional hearings continued. Highly speculative.

predictions.prediction_text: A private company attempts to colonize Mars, targeting 2026
predictions.actual_event: SpaceX has pushed Mars colonization timeline to 2028+ with no human mission yet
predictions.notes: The 2026 date was not met. SpaceX targets 2028 for first cargo missions. Direction is right, timing is off. Parody of real company Mars One, which went bankrupt.

predictions.prediction_text: Artificial intelligence and automation replace large portions of the human workforce
predictions.actual_event: Ongoing — AI job displacement accelerating in 2024-2026
predictions.notes: Mr. Burns replaces plant workers with robots. US Senate report suggests AI could displace ~100M jobs over next decade. Actively unfolding.

predictions.prediction_text: The first female President of the United States
predictions.notes: Lisa becomes president in the episode. The Trump part came true; the female president part has not yet.

predictions.prediction_text: Tom Hanks appears in a U.S. government propaganda commercial
predictions.actual_event: Tom Hanks appeared in a 2022 Biden campaign ad
predictions.notes: Broad similarity — the Simpsons Movie also featured Hanks as a government spokesperson. Weak specificity.

predictions.prediction_text: Greece defaults on its international debt obligations
predictions.actual_event: Greece became first developed country to default to IMF in 2015
predictions.notes: A news ticker reads 'Europe puts Greece on eBay' — satirizing potential EU bailout dynamics. More satire than specific prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: JCPenney files for bankruptcy
predictions.actual_event: JCPenney filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in May 2020 during COVID-19 pandemic
predictions.notes: Marge walks past a closed/derelict JCPenney in a Springfield mall. Department store decline was an observable trend even then.

predictions.prediction_text: Submersible/underwater vessel experiences a catastrophic failure
predictions.actual_event: OceanGate Titan submersible implosion, June 2023
predictions.notes: Very loose connection — a submarine/submersible scene. Weak specificity; many shows have depicted submarine disasters.

predictions.prediction_text: A Barbie-style doll becomes a massive cultural phenomenon and drives widespread 'pink mania'
predictions.actual_event: Greta Gerwig's Barbie film became highest-grossing film of 2023; pink cultural takeover
predictions.notes: Thematic parallel only — the episode critiques what doll culture teaches young girls. Not a direct prediction.

predictions.episode_title: The City of New York vs. Homer Simpson
predictions.prediction_text: Simpsons predicted the September 11 attacks via brochure showing $9 and the Twin Towers as '11'
predictions.actual_event: September 11, 2001 attacks
predictions.notes: DEBUNKED — This is a stretch requiring the viewer to interpret '$9' + Twin Towers image as '9/11'. RationalWiki and Snopes both classify this as coincidence stretched to fit.

predictions.prediction_text: Elon Musk acquires Twitter/X and dismantles its moderation policies
predictions.actual_event: Musk acquired Twitter in October 2022 for $44B, rebranded to X
predictions.notes: Episode aired same month as the acquisition. Likely written with the acquisition in progress — more commentary than prediction.

predictions.prediction_text: Recreational marijuana legalized in Canada
predictions.actual_event: Canada legalized recreational marijuana October 17, 2018
predictions.notes: Homer and friends travel to Canada to buy cheap drugs in the episode 'Midnight Rx' (S16E06). Drug decriminalization trend was already observable.

predictions.prediction_text: A three-eyed fish mutant is discovered near a nuclear power plant
predictions.actual_event: A three-eyed fish (Blinky-like) was discovered near a nuclear plant in Córdoba, Argentina in 2011
predictions.notes: The fish's exact deformity and breed didn't match Blinky exactly, but the nuclear-plant connection is eerie.

predictions.prediction_text: Holographic display technology enters mainstream use
predictions.notes: Bart receives a holographic message. 3D holographic displays exist in limited commercial form (HD3 in Best Buy stores) but are not mainstream.

predictions.prediction_text: Murder hornets terrorize the public during a period of widespread disease
predictions.actual_event: Asian Giant Hornets ('murder hornets') made U.S. headlines in spring 2020 during COVID lockdowns
predictions.notes: The timing overlap with COVID is coincidental — the hornets were already present in the Pacific Northwest. Weak causality.
